parsed_review.summary: CP00-875 (RP29.P01.M06.S04-RP29.P02.M03.S14, 150 units, risk C) closes the Commercial Readiness relationship-map/model tail and opens the service-entrypoint foundation as descriptor-only work. The pack-level claim is production_ready=false / runtime_ready=false / implementation_layer=descriptor, and that claim is fully supported. The no-runtime/no-write/no-real-data boundary is enforced at four independent layers: (1) source — policies.js and service.js are pure Object.freeze descriptor builders with zero fetch/fs-write/child_process/network/process.env usage (grep-confirmed), and every service descriptor sets writes_product_state, persists_product_state, permission_decision_written, audit_event_written, runtime_execution, and runtime_lock_acquired to false; (2) fail-closed policy — cross-tenant and missing-context route to denied_descriptor, all risk claims route to review_required, none auto-allow; (3) validators.js validateCommercialServiceMatrix asserts no-write, cross-tenant denial, and review routing; (4) tests (service.test.js + model.test.js, wired into npm test via packages/commercial/test/*.test.js) plus the canonical contract validation.valid=true with a complete no_write_attestation. Authority boundary is correct: claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false; the inspection/review-result keep Claude read-only and explicitly do NOT inherit the CP874 receipt (valid_review_receipts=[], exactly_one_valid_pack_review_required=true). Hermes patterns are control-plane only (emits_runtime_receipt=false everywhere; hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false). Review-hardening is satisfied: auth preflight passed (exit 0, 1456-byte valid JSON result); the 0-byte stdout in claude-review-result.json is the pending placeholder being filled now, not a malformed/accepted completed review. changed-file-scope correctly separates the active commercial implementation refs from unrelated dirty files (progress-control-room, workbook docs), and the pack does not claim them. One non-blocking P3: two policy branches (unsafe_deploy, customer_plan_mismatch) are covered by inspection only and not exercised by any service-matrix row or unit assertion. No P0/P1/P2 issues; verdict PASS_WITH_FINDINGS, no block to pack or this-CP goal closeout. Deterministic validation ladder (npm test, validate, spec/weighted/fullplan/goal/control-plane, git diff --check) still runs at commit time as the separate deterministic gate.

findings.title: unsafe_deploy and customer_plan_mismatch policy branches are unexercised by tests/service matrix
findings.details: evaluateCommercialReadinessPolicy routes deploy_safe===false to the 'unsafe_deploy' blocked claim (line 48) and customer_plan_matches===false to 'customer_plan_mismatch' (line 49), both resolving to review_required. However, createCommercialServiceMatrix (service.js) builds no row that toggles deploy_safe or customer_plan_matches, and neither service.test.js nor validators.js validateCommercialServiceMatrix asserts these two paths — unlike the sibling risk claims unverified_release, missing_observability, and compliance_evidence_gap, which each have a matrix row and assertions. The two branches fail closed by code inspection (they route to review_required, never allowed_descriptor), so the descriptor-only / no-write / fail-closed claim of CP00-875 is unaffected, but the regression surface for these two acceptance risks is untested.
findings.recommendation: Add a service-matrix row (e.g., unsafe_deploy via {deploy_safe:false} and customer_plan_mismatch via {customer_plan_matches:false}) and corresponding assertions in service.test.js / validateCommercialServiceMatrix so the fail-closed routing for all six COMMERCIAL_RISK_CLAIMS is locked by tests. Non-blocking for CP00-875; can be folded into the CP00-876 service tail (RP29.P02.M03.S15+).
findings.post_review_disposition: non_blocking_optional_followup — does not block CP00-875 pack or this-CP goal closeout; recommended as a test-coverage hardening item for the next service pack
